content:
🚀 seewav | Python
🎯 Primary Use Case: Creating animated video visualizations of audio waveforms for content creators, musicians, or anyone wanting to visually represent sound.
✨ Key Features: • Generates MP4 animations from audio files • Customizable video framerate • Support for stereo audio visualization • Adjustable bar colors and background color • Configurable number of bars, oversampling, and time display
📖 Summary: Seewav is a Python tool that generates animated videos from audio files by visualizing their waveforms. It allows users to customize various aspects of the animation, such as colors, resolution, and playback speed, to create engaging visual representations of sound.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

✨ cryptgeon | Svelte
🎯 Primary Use Case: Securely sharing sensitive text notes or files that automatically disappear after being viewed or after a set time, without the server having access to the content.
✨ Key Features: • Send text or files • Client-side encryption (server cannot decrypt) • View or time constraints (self-destructing) • In-memory storage (no persistence) • Obligatory dark mode support
📖 Summary: Cryptgeon is an open-source, secure note and file sharing service inspired by PrivNote. It offers client-side encryption, ensuring the server cannot access the content, and supports self-destructing notes with view or time limits. It includes a web interface, a CLI client, and a Raycast extension.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

🚀 sparkle | TypeScript
🎯 Primary Use Case: Debloating and optimizing a Windows PC through a user-friendly application interface.
✨ Key Features: • Apply Tweaks to Optimize your system • Manage All Temp files in one place • Install apps with the built-in Winget integration • Backup and Revert changes • View Basic System info
📖 Summary: Sparkle is a Windows application built with React and Electron, designed to debloat and optimize your PC. It offers features for applying system tweaks, managing temporary files, integrating with Winget for app installations, and backing up/reverting changes.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

💡 torrra | Python
🎯 Primary Use Case: Searching and downloading torrents directly from the command line with a user-friendly TUI.
✨ Key Features: • Search and download torrents from the CLI • Textual-based TUI for an interactive experience • Integration with Jackett and Prowlarr • Pause/resume download support • Customizable themes
📖 Summary: Torrra is a Python-based command-line tool that provides a text-based user interface (TUI) for searching and downloading torrents. It integrates with indexers like Jackett and Prowlarr, leverages libtorrent for downloads, and is built using the Textual framework for a rich terminal experience.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

🔥 gamewiki | Python
🎯 Primary Use Case: To provide gamers with an unobtrusive, AI-driven overlay for quick access to game information, guides, and answers without interrupting gameplay.
✨ Key Features: • One Hotkey for instant overlay access (Ctrl+Q) • AI Game Expert powered by Google Gemini with local knowledge bases • Real-time wiki overlay • Supports over 100 games for wiki access
📖 Summary: GameWiki is an AI-powered in-game overlay tool for gamers, providing instant access to wiki information and smart Q&A services powered by Google Gemini's RAG capabilities. It allows users to get answers and guides without leaving their game, enhancing the gaming experience.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source
